title: agrostis palustris var. coarctata
intro: Agrostis palustris var. coarctata, commonly known as marsh bentgrass, is a subspecies of grass native to Europe, western Asia, and North Africa. It is used in herbal medicine due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of essential oils and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of marsh bentgrass have been linked to several health benefits, including wound healing, digestive issues, and anti-arthritic effects. Marsh bentgrass can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or salve, and is traditionally used in the treatment of various health conditions, particularly skin irritations and inflammatory diseases.
